title: bus.hearth.session.permission.responded v1
description: Emitted by hearth-session's permission_publisher after the keystroke has been sent into the tmux pane. Fires regardless of whether the user approved or denied; the keystroke itself (approve=1, deny=N) is already consumed. Consumers use this to dismiss the permission card and update session state in the phone UI.

correlation_id.description: UUID v4 token matching the correlation_id from the corresponding permission.requested event.

decision.description: The user's final decision; deny_with_feedback includes a feedback string sent as subsequent keystrokes. 'resolved' is emitted by the server's stale-prompt reaper (not a user action) when a prompt disappears — answered at the desktop, expired, or the session closed — so consumers dismiss the now-orphaned permission card; no keystroke is sent for 'resolved'.

feedback.description: Free-text feedback typed by the user when denying with feedback; passed as additional keystrokes after the deny key.

responded_at.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp when the response was recorded.
